---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
---

```{r}
#| label: Load libraries
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(jsonlite)
library(rstatix)
```

```{r}
#| label: Load data into data frame
#| include: false
data <- read.csv('sampledata.csv')
expl_data <- read.csv('expldata.csv')
expl_data <- expl_data |> select(-score)
data <- merge(data, expl_data, "subject_id")
```

T test prep:
```{r}
data_correct = data |>
    filter(correct == 1)
data_incorrect = data |>
    filter(correct == 0)
```

H1: Early confidence in identity => low score
```{r}
t.test(data_correct$max_early_theory, data_incorrect$max_early_theory)
cor.test(data$max_early_theory, data$score, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = max_early_theory, score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       H1: Maximum Early Theory vs Score") + xlab("Maximum Early Theory Confidence") + ylab("Score")
```

H2: Time & effort => high score

Time:
```{r}
t.test(data_correct$minutes, data_incorrect$minutes)
cor.test(data$minutes, data$score, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = minutes, score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       H2A: Time vs Score") + xlab("Time (Minutes)") + ylab("Score")
```

Word count:
```{r}
t.test(data_correct$minutes, data_incorrect$minutes)
cor.test(data$wordcount, data$score, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = wordcount, score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       H2B: Word Count vs Score") + xlab("Notes Word Count") + ylab("Score")
```

H3: Ambiguity tolerance => lower max early theory => better performance
```{r}
cor.test(data$uncertainty, data$max_early_theory, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = uncertainty, max_early_theory)) + geom_point()
t.test(data_correct$uncertainty, data_incorrect$uncertainty)
cor.test(data$uncertainty, data$score, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = uncertainty, score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       H3B: Uncertainty Intolerance vs Score") + xlab("Uncertainty Intolerance") + ylab("Score")
```

H4: Need for cognition => higher word count & time => better performance

```{r}
cor.test(data$cognition, data$minutes, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = cognition, minutes)) + geom_point()
cor.test(data$cognition, data$wordcount, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = cognition, wordcount)) + geom_point()
t.test(data_correct$cognition, data_incorrect$score)
cor.test(data$cognition, data$score, method = 'pearson')
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = cognition, score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       H4C: Need for Cognition vs Score") + xlab("Need for Cognition") + ylab("Score")
```


EXPLORATORY

```{r}
#(subject_id-10)/100 is to add consistent jitter
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = timeline+((subject_id-10)/100), y = score)) + geom_point() 
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = note_drop+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = rolling+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = note_update+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       Note Updating") + xlab("Note Updating") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = accident..0.1.2.+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = explicit+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = character+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = simultaneous+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       Concurrent Theory Generation") + xlab("Concurrent Theory Generation") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = avoidance+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = fact_theory..0.1.2.+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       Fact-based vs Theory-based Note Taking") + xlab("Fact-based (0) vs Theory-based (2) Note Taking") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = generation+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       High Volume Theory Generation") + xlab("Theory Generation") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = means_motive..0.1.2.+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       Attention to Means vs Motive") + xlab("Attention to Means (0) vs Motive (2)") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = reflection+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = final_confidence+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = broad+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = partial_evi+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = partial_id+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = complete+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point() + ggtitle("       Complete Theory Generation") + xlab("Complete Theory Generation") + ylab("Score")
ggplot(data = data, aes(x = confidence.0.1.2.+((subject_id-10)/100), score)) + geom_point()
```


```{r}
t.test(filter(data, note_drop == 0)$score, filter(data, note_drop == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, rolling == 0)$score, filter(data, rolling == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, note_update == 0)$score, filter(data, note_update == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, accident..0.1.2. == 0$score), filter(data, accident..0.1.2. == 2)$score)
t.test(filter(data, explicit == 0)$score, filter(data, explicit == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, character == 0), filter(data, character == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, simultaneous == 0$score), filter(data, simultaneous == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, avoidance == 0)$score, filter(data, avoidance == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, fact_theory..0.1.2. == 0), filter(data, fact_theory..0.1.2. == 2)$score)
t.test(filter(data, generation == 0)$score, filter(data, generation == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, means_motive..0.1.2. == 0)$score, filter(data, means_motive..0.1.2. == 2)$score)
t.test(filter(data, reflection == 0)$score, filter(data, reflection == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, broad == 0)$score, filter(data, broad == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, partial_evi == 0)$score, filter(data, partial_evi == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data$score, partial_id == 0)$score, filter(data, partial_id == 1)$score)
t.test(filter(data, complete == 1)$score, filter(data, complete == 0)$score)
t.test(filter(data, confidence == 0)$score, filter(data, confidence == 1)$score)
cor.test(data$score, data$final_confidence, method = 'pearson')
```
